name: Singapore
localTips: Tap a contactless bank card or phone wallet directly on MRT gates and buses — there's no need to buy a separate tourist transit card.
Chewing gum can't be sold here and eating or drinking on the MRT carries a real fine — save snacks for the platform or street.
Hawker centres are the best-value meals in the city; a tissue packet on a seat means it's 'chope-d' (reserved), so find an empty table instead.
Sudden tropical downpours hit most afternoons — most MRT stations connect underground to malls, so you can shop your way across town and stay dry.
Alcohol is taxed heavily in bars and restaurants; hawker centre beer stalls or a BYO evening at Dempsey Hill beat hotel prices by a mile.

activities.title: Merlion Park & Marina Bay Waterfront
activities.description: Snap the half-lion, half-fish spouting into the bay, then walk the promenade past Marina Bay Sands and the ArtScience Museum lotus, lit up after dark.

activities.title: Gardens by the Bay Supertree Grove
activities.description: Wander free beneath the 50-metre Supertrees and catch the nightly Garden Rhapsody light-and-sound show — the Cloud Forest domes cost extra, the grove itself doesn't.

activities.title: Jewel Changi Rain Vortex
activities.description: The world's tallest indoor waterfall drops through a mall you don't need a boarding pass to enter — the surrounding Shiseido Forest Valley is free and air-conditioned.

activities.title: Henderson Waves & Southern Ridges walk
activities.description: Singapore's highest pedestrian bridge undulates 36 metres above Henderson Road — part of the 10km Southern Ridges trail linking Mount Faber to Kent Ridge Park.

activities.title: MacRitchie Reservoir TreeTop Walk
activities.description: A free suspension bridge strung 25 metres above the rainforest canopy, reached by an hour's hike from the reservoir — look for long-tailed macaques and monitor lizards.

activities.title: Chinatown streets & Sri Mariamman Temple
activities.description: Wander Pagoda and Temple Streets past incense shops and dried-goods stalls to Singapore's oldest Hindu temple, its gopuram tower carved with hundreds of technicolour deities.

activities.title: Little India & Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple
activities.description: Marigold garlands, spice sacks and gold shops line Serangoon Road around the goddess Kali's fierce, brightly painted temple — busiest and most fragrant on weekend mornings.

activities.title: Kampong Glam, Sultan Mosque & Haji Lane
activities.description: The golden dome of Sultan Mosque anchors Singapore's Malay quarter — duck into Haji Lane's narrow strip of independent boutiques and street art right behind it.

activities.title: Fort Canning Park
activities.description: A hilltop park layered with 700 years of history — a spice garden, the ASEAN Sculpture Garden and the gates of a WWII command bunker, minutes from Orchard Road.

activities.title: Tiong Bahru art deco estate
activities.description: Singapore's oldest public housing estate, all curved 1930s balconies and spiral staircases — now threaded with indie bookshops and bakeries between the original kopitiams.

activities.title: East Coast Park beach path
activities.description: 8.5km of sand, seafood shacks and bike paths facing the shipping lanes — rent a bike at any of the park's kiosks or just watch the container ships queue for port.

activities.title: Singapore Botanic Gardens free lawns
activities.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site since 1859 — Swan Lake, the Rain Forest fragment and the Bandstand lawns are free; only the National Orchid Garden charges admission.

activities.title: Katong & Joo Chiat peranakan shophouses
activities.description: Pastel shophouses with hand-painted tiles line Koon Seng Road — the Peranakan heartland, where kopitiams sell laksa a few doors from century-old beading shops.

activities.title: Clarke Quay & Boat Quay riverside walk
activities.description: Restored godowns turned bars and restaurants glow along the Singapore River — walk it after dark when the Cavenagh Bridge and skyline reflections come alive.

activities.title: Tiong Bahru Market kopitiam breakfast
activities.description: Locals queue before 8am for chwee kueh and lor mee at this two-storey hawker centre — the wet market stalls downstairs sell the produce the kopitiams cook upstairs.
activities.insiderNote: Tian Tian Lor Mee and Jian Bo Shui Kueh are the queues that matter; go before 9am before the good stalls sell out.

activities.title: Chinatown Complex Food Centre
activities.description: Singapore's largest hawker centre — over 200 stalls including Michelin-starred Liao Fan Hawker Chan's soya sauce chicken, still under S$5 a plate.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the ground-floor tourist stalls and head to the 2nd-floor cooked-food centre where the aunties and uncles actually eat.

activities.title: Hidden speakeasy nightcap
activities.description: Down an unmarked stairwell off Ann Siang Hill, bartenders build drinks around Southeast Asian ingredients like pandan and tamarind — no sign, just a door.
activities.insiderNote: Look for the plain black door with a small brass plaque near the top of Ann Siang Road — reservations recommended after 9pm.

activities.title: Pulau Ubin cycling & kampong chicken rice
activities.description: A ten-minute bumboat from Changi Point drops you on an island frozen in 1960s kampong life — rent a bike at the jetty and ride to Chek Jawa wetlands.
activities.insiderNote: Bumboats leave when they fill up (12 people) for a flat S$4 fare — no fixed schedule, just show up at the jetty.

activities.title: Dempsey Hill colonial enclave
activities.description: Former British army barracks turned into a leafy cluster of restaurants and wine bars behind the Botanic Gardens — quieter and less touristy than Clarke Quay.
activities.insiderNote: PS.Café's original garden branch is here, but the real find is Anti:dote's low-key gin bar next door.

activities.title: Adam Road Food Centre
activities.description: A modest hawker centre next to the Botanic Gardens that locals rate above flashier tourist spots — nasi lemak, satay and rojak stalls with decades-long queues.
activities.insiderNote: Selera Rasa's nasi lemak is the one with the queue; go before noon or after 2pm to skip the office-lunch crowd.

activities.title: Keong Saik shophouse bar-hopping
activities.description: A former red-light district turned Singapore's densest strip of natural-wine bars and cocktail dens, all in restored pre-war shophouses.
activities.insiderNote: Jigger & Pony started the neighbourhood's cocktail scene; walk five minutes in any direction for a dozen more without a queue.

activities.title: Katong kueh and laksa institution
activities.description: 328 Katong Laksa's cut-noodle laksa (eaten with a spoon, no chopsticks needed) started the neighbourhood's fame — follow it with kueh from Kim Choo a few doors down.
activities.insiderNote: Ask for it 'less spicy' if you want to taste the coconut broth — locals genuinely order it fiery.

activities.title: Cloud Forest & Flower Dome tickets
activities.description: A 35-metre indoor waterfall inside a climate-controlled mountain, plus ever-changing floral displays in the Flower Dome next door — book combo tickets to skip the queue.
activities.bookingQuery: Gardens by the Bay Cloud Forest Flower Dome ticket Singapore

activities.title: Singapore Zoo
activities.description: An open-concept zoo in the rainforest where moats replace bars — catch the Splash Safari sea lion show and the orangutan breakfast experience.

activities.title: Night Safari tram tour
activities.description: The world's first nocturnal zoo — a tram ride past free-roaming hyenas and tigers under low red lighting, followed by a walking trail past leopards.

activities.title: Bird Paradise walk-through aviaries
activities.description: Eight walk-through aviaries recreating rainforests from four continents — hand-feed lorikeets and watch the flamingo lagoon at feeding time.

activities.title: Universal Studios Singapore
activities.description: Southeast Asia's only Universal park — ride the world's tallest duelling rollercoasters on Sentosa and walk through a life-size Jurassic Park lagoon.

activities.title: Singapore Flyer observation wheel
activities.description: A 165-metre-high wheel with air-conditioned capsules — a full rotation takes 30 minutes, long enough to spot Malaysia and Indonesia on a clear day.

activities.title: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark observation deck
activities.description: The rooftop deck of the three-tower hotel, with the infinity pool visible below (guests only) and a 360-degree skyline view best booked for sunset.
activities.bookingQuery: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark observation deck ticket

activities.title: ArtScience Museum exhibitions
activities.description: Housed in the lotus-shaped building beside Marina Bay Sands — rotating digital-art and science exhibitions, including past runs of teamLab's immersive rooms.

activities.title: Chinatown heritage & hawker food tour
activities.description: A guide walks you through Chinatown's temples, shophouses and hawker stalls, ordering dishes you'd never pick blind off a menu you can't read.
activities.bookingQuery: Chinatown food and heritage walking tour Singapore

activities.title: Singapore River bumboat cruise
activities.description: A restored bumboat glides from Clarke Quay past the Merlion to Marina Bay, the lit-up skyline reflecting in the water — the classic first-night orientation.
